name: Piney Woods Hush Puppies
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix all dry ingredients.
2. Beat the milk and egg together.
3. Combine with the dry ingredients until the batter holds its shape when picked up in a spoon. If it is too soft, add more cornmeal.
4. Drop by spoonfuls into 350°F fat.
5. Cook for about 1 1/2 minutes on each side.
6. Allow oil to heat a few seconds after removing a batch.
7. Delicious freshly cooked and hot; however, leftover hush puppies freeze well. When ready to serve frozen hush puppies, place them on an oven rack in preheated 250°F until very hot and crisp.
Makes about 48 hush puppies (2-inch round). Hints on frying: Using a small diameter heavy saucepan allows using a minimum oil; a 5 or 6 inch pan with oil 1 1/2 inches deep and heated to 350°F will cook three hush puppies in about 2 1/2 minutes. When batter consistency is correct and oil is at 350°F, hush puppies will become firm, round shapes almost as soon as they enter the hot oil. If cooked in oil that is too hot, they will not cook in the center.
Serving suggestions: Especially good served with fried catfish, trout, shrimp, oysters, chicken, or chicken-fried steak.
